assembly_instructions: Read MiniMax-M2.7 as the LIKELY next step beyond MiniMax-M1: push the lightning-attention philosophy further. 96 transformer blocks climb the orange residual pillar. Pattern: 11 LIGHTNING (linear with learnable gating, Mamba-style) blocks for every 1 SOFTMAX (sliding-window 4096) block — was 7:1 in M1 with full-context softmax. Lightning blocks are pale-amber; softmax blocks are deep-crimson. Lightning attention now has a LEARNABLE GATE on the state update (Mamba-style decay), not just a fixed feature map — this is the projected upgrade. Cyan torus at X=-2.5 is the lightning state; small amber column at X=-3.5 is the much smaller softmax KV cache (only 8 layers × 4096-token window). To the right at X=6 sits the per-layer MoE FFN (64 routed experts, top-3, 1 shared). Above the stack at X=2/Y=9.5 is the next-gen RL trainer (projected MARS — Multi-Agent Reasoning with Self-play). The translucent slab at X=8 marks the parallelism + 4M-context-input plan. Click any part for details. THIS IS A FORWARD-LOOKING PROJECTION — numbers should be read as plausible extrapolation, not fact.

info.summary: PROJECTED next-generation reasoning flagship from MiniMax, extrapolated from MiniMax-Text-01 → M1 research direction. No public paper at scene time. Best guess: pushes lightning attention further — 11:1 LIGHTNING:SOFTMAX ratio (was 7:1 in M1), learnable gating on the state update (Mamba-style), softmax replaced with sliding-window 4096 (full-context dropped). 96 layers, ~720B total / ~55B activated, 64 routed + 1 shared expert top-3 (was 32+1 top-2), 4M native input context, 200K output budget, post-trained with a projected MARS (Multi-Agent Reasoning with Self-play) RL successor to CISPO.
info.description:
MiniMax-M2.7 is presented here as a research-trajectory PROJECTION, not a paper. Where MiniMax-M1 was 7:1 lightning:softmax over 80 layers with full-context softmax and 32 experts top-2, M2.7 likely (i) PUSHES the lightning ratio to 11:1 — the M1 paper hinted that recall could be preserved with fewer softmax layers if the softmax is smarter, (ii) replaces full-context softmax with SLIDING-WINDOW softmax (4096-token window) — an operational simplification that bounds KV memory cleanly, (iii) adds LEARNABLE GATING to the lightning kernel (Mamba/Mamba-2 style decay) — measurably better long-range recall than fixed-feature-map linear attention, (iv) doubles routed-expert count (32 → 64) at top-3 (was top-2) for ~50% more active capacity, (v) extends native input context 1M → 4M with a multi-stage curriculum, (vi) succeeds CISPO with a projected 'MARS' (Multi-Agent Reasoning with Self-play) algorithm that matches multiple policies against each other on math/code/agent tasks.

Design rationale, in one line each:
• 11:1 lightning ratio: KV memory at 4M ctx is dominated almost entirely by the 8 softmax layers — and they only carry a 4096 window.
• Mamba-style gating: the single biggest reason linear attention finally matches softmax on long-range recall.
• Sliding-window softmax: bounds the KV cache regardless of input length — an operational win at 4M ctx.
• 64 experts top-3: more active capacity per token without proportionally growing all-to-all traffic.
• MARS RL: M1's CISPO solved long-rollout stability; the next problem is reasoning-via-debate and self-play.

facts.label: post-training RL
facts.value: MARS — Multi-Agent Reasoning with Self-play (projected successor to CISPO)

facts.label: RL reward sources
facts.value: Math, code, agentic tool-use, SWE, multi-agent debate

facts.label: precision
facts.value: BF16 weights + FP4/FP8 GEMMs (projected FP4 expert QAT)

comparisons.delta: Same family. Projected M2.7 grows: total params 456B → 720B, activated 45.9B → 55B (~20% bigger compute / token). Layers stay similar (80 → 96). Lightning:softmax ratio pushed 7:1 → 11:1 (more linear, less softmax). Softmax full-context → sliding-window 4096. Lightning kernel gains learnable gating (Mamba-style), the single biggest known fix to linear attention's long-range recall problem. Experts double (32 → 64), top-k lifts 2 → 3. Input context 1M → 4M; output 80K → 200K. CISPO RL → projected MARS RL.

Two generations of distance. Text-01 introduced the 7:1 hybrid. M2.7 keeps the hybrid but pushes ratio, replaces fixed-feature-map linear attention with gated linear, replaces full-ctx softmax with sliding-window softmax, scales experts and depth, and adds reasoning-specific RL post-training.

Both are hybrid-attention reasoning MoEs but at OPPOSITE extremes. Qwen3.6 stays at 3:1 LINEAR:SOFTMAX with full-context softmax — favours short-context quality. M2.7 goes 11:1 with sliding-window-only softmax — favours million-token context economics. M2.7 uses Mamba-style gated linear attention; Qwen3.6 uses Gated DeltaNet (delta-rule). Different bets on the linear-attention design space.

V4-Pro keeps full softmax (CSA + HCA hybrid + lightning indexer + top-k=1024). M2.7 takes the orthogonal route: 92% LINEAR attention layers + 8% sliding-window softmax. At 4M context M2.7's KV is dominated by 8 layers × 4096-token windows ≈ ~1 GB / sequence; V4-Pro at 1M ctx is ~4 GiB / sequence. Different cost profiles: V4 is read-bandwidth-bound on the indexer GEMM, M2.7 is sequential-state-update-bound on the gated lightning layers.

parts.name: Input Tokens (V≈200K)
parts.role: Token-ID stream from MiniMax's BPE.

parts.name: Embedding (V≈200K, d=6144)
parts.role: Tied embedding / LM head.

parts.name: Residual Stream (d=6144, 96 layers)
parts.role: Pre-norm RMSNorm residual carry through 96 layers.

parts.name: × 96 Layers (11 LIGHTNING : 1 SLIDING-SOFTMAX)
parts.role: Marker: 88 lightning + 8 sliding-softmax. Pattern repeats 11 lightning : 1 softmax × 8.

parts.name: Block 1 — LIGHTNING (gated)
parts.role: Gated lightning attention block. Mamba-style learnable decay on the state update — the projected upgrade over M1's fixed-feature lightning.

parts.name: Block 2 — LIGHTNING
parts.role: Lightning block 2.

parts.name: Block 3 — LIGHTNING
parts.role: Lightning block 3.

parts.name: Block 4 — LIGHTNING
parts.role: Lightning block 4.

parts.name: Block 5 — LIGHTNING
parts.role: Lightning block 5.

parts.name: Block 12 — SOFTMAX (sliding-window 4096)
parts.role: Sliding-window softmax block — only attends to the previous 4096 tokens. Restores associative recall lost by linear attention. (Layers 6–11 lightning, elided.)

parts.name: Block 13 — LIGHTNING
parts.role: Lightning block 13. Pattern resumes.

parts.name: Block 14 — LIGHTNING
parts.role: Lightning block 14.

parts.name: Block 24 — SOFTMAX
parts.role: Second softmax block. (Layers 15–23 lightning, elided.)

parts.name: Block 96 — SOFTMAX (final)
parts.role: Final softmax block before LM head.

parts.name: Lightning · Pre-RMSNorm
parts.role: Pre-norm before the lightning sublayer.

parts.name: Lightning · QKV + decay-gate Proj (Δ_t)
parts.role: Project x to Q, K, V plus a per-token DECAY GATE Δ_t ∈ (0,1) (Mamba-style). Δ_t controls how fast old state is forgotten.
parts.algorithm:
Q, K, V = projections(x)
Δ_t     = sigmoid(x @ W_decay)   # per-token decay (Mamba)

parts.name: Gated Lightning Kernel (Mamba-style)
parts.role: Updated lightning kernel with Mamba-2 style learnable decay. Per-token cost is O(d_k·d_v), independent of context length, but the state now decays selectively rather than uniformly — the single biggest fix to linear attention's long-range recall.
parts.memory_footprint: S: d_k × d_v per layer per sequence ≈ 1–2 MB / sequence / layer
parts.algorithm:
# per token (decode):
S = (1 - Δ_t) * S + Δ_t * outer(K_t, V_t)   # gated update
out_t = Q_t @ S
# prefill: scan-parallel (Mamba-2 chunk-scan kernel)
parts.bottleneck: Sequential along time at decode — chunked-scan prefill recovers parallelism for long inputs.
improvement_ideas: FP8 state with periodic FP16 stabilisation.
Multi-head gated lightning with shared keys.

parts.name: Lightning State (S, fixed-size)
parts.role: Per-sequence per-layer recurrent state. Size INDEPENDENT of context length — what makes 4M context affordable.
parts.memory_footprint: ≈ 1–2 MB per sequence per layer, regardless of T

parts.name: Lightning · O-Projection
parts.role: Output projection back to d=6144.

parts.name: Softmax · QKV Proj (GQA 64/8)
parts.role: QKV for the 1-in-12 sliding-window softmax layers.
parts.algorithm:
Q, K, V = projections(x)
Q, K = apply_rope(Q, K)
out = flash_attn(Q, K, V, mask=causal_sliding(window=4096))

parts.name: Softmax KV Cache (8 layers × 4096 window)
parts.role: Tiny GQA(8) KV cache: 8 softmax layers × 4096-token window. Constant in input length.
parts.memory_footprint: ≈ 4 KB / token / layer × 4096 × 8 layers ≈ 130 MiB / sequence — TINY at 4M ctx

parts.name: Top-3 Softmax Router (capacity)
parts.role: Per-token softmax over 64 experts, top-3. Token-choice with capacity factor; over-capacity tokens fall through to the shared expert.

parts.name: Routed Experts (64, top-3, d_ff=9216)
parts.role: Per-MoE-layer bank of 64 SwiGLU experts (d_ff=9216). 3 active per token (was 2 in M1).
parts.memory_footprint: 64 × 3·d·d_ff × BF16 ≈ 72 GiB / layer; 96 layers ≈ 6.7 TiB total routed weights

parts.name: Shared Expert (×1)
parts.role: Always-on overflow + common-computation expert.

parts.name: Expert All-to-All
parts.role: NCCL all-to-all dispatch + combine. Bigger than M1 because top-3 (was top-2).

parts.name: FP4 Expert Weights (projected QAT)
parts.role: Projected FP4 quantisation-aware training on routed experts (cross-pollination from DeepSeek-V4).

parts.name: LM Head (tied)
parts.role: Tied projection back to ~200K vocab.

parts.name: LM Loss
parts.role: Pre-training next-token loss.

parts.name: MARS RL Trainer (projected: Multi-Agent Reasoning + Self-play)
parts.role: PROJECTED successor to CISPO. Multiple policy copies debate / cooperate on math, code, agent and SWE tasks; rewards include outcome correctness AND inter-agent agreement. Designed for very long (200K) reasoning rollouts.
parts.algorithm:
for round in range(R):
    agents = [policy.copy() for _ in range(N)]
    rollouts, debate_log = self_play(agents, prompts)
    rewards = score_outcome(rollouts) + λ * agreement(debate_log)
    loss = clipped_is_loss(rollouts, rewards)
    policy.update(loss)

parts.name: Reward Models (math / code / agent / SWE / debate)
parts.role: Domain-specific rewards used by MARS, plus the new debate-agreement reward.

parts.name: AdamW (or Muon — projected)
parts.role: Standard AdamW; Muon adoption is plausible given DeepSeek-V4's results.

parts.name: TP × EP × PP × DP
parts.role: Multi-axis parallelism plan. Lightning layers need sequence-parallel chunked-scan prefill + state-passing across PP boundaries.

parts.name: Pre-training + Long-Output MARS RL (4M ctx curriculum)
parts.role: Projected pre-training + multi-stage RL with long-output (200K) rollouts.
